heading: Investigation of foreign telecommunications trade barriers
label: 3103 Investigation of foreign telecommunications trade barriers

children.heading: In general
children.label: a In general
children.text: The Trade Representative shall conduct an investigation to identify priority foreign countries. Such investigation shall be concluded by no later than the date that is 5 months after .August 23, 1988

children.heading: Factors to be taken into account
children.label: b Factors to be taken into account

children.text: the nature and significance of the acts, policies, and practices that deny mutually advantageous market opportunities to telecommunications products and services of United States firms;
the economic benefits (actual and potential) accruing to foreign firms from open access to the United States market;
the potential size of the market of a foreign country for telecommunications products and services of United States firms;
the potential to increase United States exports of telecommunications products and services, either directly or through the establishment of a beneficial precedent; and
measurable progress being made to eliminate the objectionable acts, policies, or practices.

children.label: A
children.text: revoke the identification of any priority foreign country that was made under this section, or

children.label: B
children.text: identify any foreign country as a priority foreign country under this section,

The Trade Representative shall include in the semiannual report submitted to the Congress under a detailed explanation of the reasons for the revocation under paragraph (1) of this subsection of any identification of any foreign country as a priority foreign country.section 2419(3) of this title

children.heading: Report to Congress
children.label: d Report to Congress
children.text: By no later than the date that is 30 days after the date on which the investigation conducted under subsection (a) is completed, the United States Trade Representative shall submit a report on the investigation to the President and to appropriate committees of the Congress.
